meta.project: ILM — Interactive Learning Material (V1, workflow v2)
meta.diagram_node: Skill 1: concept-extraction -> Keep only NEW concepts -> Eval gate 1
meta.note: v0.3.0: realigned to updated rubrics.json — added the new clubbing rules 7.0 (<=2 subheadings/unit), 8.0 (<=3 commands/unit), 9.0 (no clubbing of separate simple headings), each with a bad/flag boundary case (CPT-08/09/10) and a good/pass twin (CPT-08b/09b/10b), plus supporting spans S6-S8; Granularity (4.0, CPT-04) now references these clubbing allowances.

source_spans.S1: An AI agent is a program that uses a language model to decide which actions to take. It runs in a loop: observe the current state, choose a tool, execute it, and feed the result back into the model. The loop ends when the model decides the task is complete.
source_spans.S2: Tool calling lets the model request that the host program run a specific function, such as a web search or a calculator, and return the result to the model.
source_spans.S3: A system prompt is a fixed instruction given to the model before the conversation starts. It sets the agent's role, rules, and output format.
source_spans.S4: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) fetches relevant passages from a document store and adds them to the prompt, so the model can answer using information it was never trained on. The model's weights are not changed.
source_spans.S5: A human-in-the-loop gate requires a person to approve the agent's output before it takes effect.
source_spans.S6: Heading 'Python Functions' has three subheadings: 'Defining a function' (the def keyword and body), 'Calling a function' (passing arguments), and 'Return values' (how a function hands a result back). Each subheading teaches a distinct mechanic.
source_spans.S7: Heading 'Inspecting a Git repo' walks through four commands: git status (what changed), git log (history), git diff (line-level changes), and git show (a single commit's contents).
source_spans.S8: Two separate top-level headings, each with no subheadings: 'What is a variable' (a named box that holds a value) and 'What is a constant' (a named value that never changes).

cases.edge_case: Partial overlap, not exact duplicate — extracted concept is a superset of an already-taught one

candidate_concept.title: Prompting and system prompts
candidate_concept.summary: How to write prompts, plus the special fixed prompt that configures an agent.

cases.failure_mode: Half the concept ('how to write prompts') re-teaches 'prompting basics' from a past RM. Only the system-prompt half is new. Graders that only check exact title matches will miss this.
cases.judge_instructions: Compare the candidate concept against the past-concepts list at the idea level, not the title level. Flag if any substantial portion of the candidate re-covers a past concept, even when bundled with new material.

metadata.notes: Negative case: candidate bundles a new idea (system prompts) with an already-taught one (prompting basics). Expected to be flagged at idea-level, not title-level.

cases.edge_case: Concept is plausible for the domain and adjacent to the RM, but the RM never discusses it

candidate_concept.title: Fine-tuning the agent's model
candidate_concept.summary: Adapting model weights to the agent's task domain.

cases.failure_mode: Fine-tuning sounds like it belongs in an agents doc — S4 even mentions weights ('weights are not changed') — but no span teaches it. The mention of a word is not coverage of a concept.
cases.judge_instructions: Flag unless at least one span explicitly discusses the concept itself. A span that merely contains a related keyword does not count as grounding.

metadata.notes: Negative case: plausible, domain-adjacent concept with no supporting span. A related keyword appearing in a span is not the same as the span teaching the concept.

cases.edge_case: Output misses exactly one concept, and the missed one is the least prominent in the RM
cases.failure_mode: Misses 'human-in-the-loop gate' (one short span, S5). Near-miss coverage is the realistic failure — extraction rarely misses the headline concept, it misses the one-paragraph concept.
cases.judge_instructions: Reviewer compares against the gold list. Any missed gold concept = flag, regardless of how minor the concept appears in the RM.

metadata.notes: Negative case: extraction drops the least prominent gold concept (S5). Human grader; any missed gold concept flags regardless of prominence.

cases.edge_case: Two genuinely related ideas bundled as one concept — defensible-sounding, still wrong

candidate_concept.title: Tool calling and RAG
candidate_concept.summary: How agents get outside information: by calling tools and by retrieving documents.

cases.failure_mode: The bundle has a unifying theme ('getting outside information'), which makes it look like one concept. But each half needs its own explanation, analogy, and quiz — it is two teachable units. These are two separate top-level mechanisms (S2, S4), not a permitted clubbing under cases 7.0/8.0/9.0.
cases.judge_instructions: Flag if the concept requires teaching two separable mechanisms beyond the clubbing allowances in cases 7.0 (<=2 subheadings of one main heading), 8.0 (<=3 commands under one heading), and 9.0 (a single simple heading). Test: could a quiz question about one half be answered without the other half? If yes, and the bundle is not a permitted clubbing, it is two concepts.

metadata.notes: Negative case: two separable mechanisms (tool calling, RAG) bundled under a unifying theme. Each half is its own teachable unit; expected to be flagged.

cases.edge_case: Same idea extracted twice under non-matching titles

candidate_output.title: The agent loop
candidate_output.summary: Observe, choose a tool, execute, feed result back.

candidate_output.title: How agents decide and act
candidate_output.summary: The cycle of observing state and taking actions until done.

cases.failure_mode: Zero title overlap, near-100% idea overlap, same source span. String-similarity dedup passes this; semantic dedup must catch it.
cases.judge_instructions: Compare concepts pairwise at the idea level. Flag if any two would produce substantially the same explanation.

metadata.notes: Negative case: same idea (S1 agent loop) extracted twice under non-matching titles. String-similarity dedup misses it; semantic dedup must flag it.

cases.edge_case: Title is accurate but too broad to be specific

candidate_concept.title: Working with AI
candidate_concept.summary: A human-in-the-loop gate requires a person to approve outputs before they take effect.

cases.failure_mode: 'Working with AI' is not false, just vague — it could title any concept in the doc. Vagueness is the edge; outright mismatch is the easy case.
cases.judge_instructions: Flag if the title could plausibly apply to two or more other concepts in the same RM. Titles must be specific enough to identify the concept uniquely.

metadata.notes: Negative case: accurate but over-broad title that could label any concept in the doc. Vagueness (not mismatch) is the edge being tested.

cases.edge_case: One main heading whose three subheadings are clubbed into a single concept unit — one over the 2-subheading allowance

candidate_concept.title: Python Functions
candidate_concept.summary: Defining a function, calling a function, and return values, all in one unit.

cases.failure_mode: The main heading 'Python Functions' has three subheadings. The rubric allows clubbing at most 2 subheadings into one concept unit; clubbing all 3 exceeds that, so it must be split (e.g. 2 + 1). Boundary case: 2 would pass, 3 flags.
cases.judge_instructions: Count the subheadings of the same main heading clubbed into this concept unit. Pass if <=2 are clubbed; flag if >2 subheadings of the main heading are clubbed into a single unit.

metadata.notes: Negative case for the new clubbing rule (rubric 7.0): >2 subheadings of one main heading clubbed into a single Learning Concept Unit.

cases.edge_case: Exactly two subheadings of one main heading clubbed into a single concept unit — at the 2-subheading allowance

candidate_concept.title: Defining and calling a function
candidate_concept.summary: How to define a function and how to call it, taught together as one unit.

cases.judge_instructions: Count the subheadings of the same main heading clubbed into this concept unit. The rubric permits clubbing up to 2 subheadings; exactly 2 sits on the allowance and must pass. A judge that flags this is over-strict.

metadata.notes: Positive boundary twin of CPT-08 (rubric 7.0): exactly 2 subheadings clubbed is the permitted maximum and must pass.

cases.edge_case: Four commands under one heading clubbed into a single concept unit — one over the 3-command allowance

candidate_concept.title: Inspecting a Git repo
candidate_concept.summary: git status, git log, git diff, and git show taught together as one unit.

cases.failure_mode: The heading lists four commands. The rubric allows clubbing at most 3 commands into one concept unit; clubbing all 4 exceeds that, so it must be split. Boundary case: 3 would pass, 4 flags.
cases.judge_instructions: Count the commands under the same heading clubbed into this concept unit. Pass if <=3 are clubbed; flag if >3 commands of the heading are clubbed into a single unit.

metadata.notes: Negative case for the new clubbing rule (rubric 8.0): >3 commands of one heading clubbed into a single Learning Concept Unit.

cases.edge_case: Exactly three commands under one heading clubbed into a single concept unit — at the 3-command allowance

candidate_concept.title: Inspecting a Git repo
candidate_concept.summary: git status, git log, and git diff taught together as one unit.

cases.judge_instructions: Count the commands under the same heading clubbed into this concept unit. The rubric permits clubbing up to 3 commands; exactly 3 sits on the allowance and must pass. A judge that flags this is over-strict.

metadata.notes: Positive boundary twin of CPT-09 (rubric 8.0): exactly 3 commands clubbed is the permitted maximum and must pass.

cases.criterion: One main simple heading with no subheadings or subconcepts.
cases.edge_case: Two separate simple headings (each with no subheadings) clubbed into one concept unit

candidate_concept.title: Variables and constants
candidate_concept.summary: What a variable is and what a constant is, combined into one unit.

cases.failure_mode: These are two separate simple top-level headings, each with no subheadings. The rubric says a single simple heading is one Learning Concept Unit; clubbing two distinct simple headings together is not permitted and must be split into two units.
cases.judge_instructions: A single simple heading with no subheadings is one concept unit (pass). Flag if >=2 separate simple headings (each with no subheadings) are clubbed into a single Learning Concept Unit.

metadata.notes: Negative case for the new clubbing rule (rubric 9.0): two separate simple headings clubbed as one Learning Concept Unit.

cases.criterion: One main simple heading with no subheadings or subconcepts.
cases.edge_case: A single simple heading with no subheadings treated as one concept unit

candidate_concept.title: What is a variable
candidate_concept.summary: A named box that holds a value.

cases.judge_instructions: A single simple heading with no subheadings is exactly one Learning Concept Unit and must pass. Flag only when >=2 separate simple headings are clubbed together; this unit clubs none, so a judge that flags it is over-strict.

metadata.notes: Positive twin of CPT-10 (rubric 9.0): one simple heading treated as a single unit, the permitted case, must pass.

cases.edge_case: Clean extraction — calibrates the judge against over-flagging

candidate_concept.title: Human-in-the-loop approval gate
candidate_concept.summary: A required human approval step before an agent's output takes effect.

cases.judge_instructions: This concept is new, grounded in S5, single-idea, and specifically titled. A judge that flags this is over-strict; recalibrate the judge prompt.

metadata.notes: Positive anchor: new, grounded, single-idea, specifically titled. Calibrates the judge against over-flagging.
